mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: neurological symptoms (restlessness, vertigo, diminished movement), liver involvement (hepatic steatosis, hyperlipidemia)
cardiac involvement (right ventricular hypertrophy, pulmonary arterial hypertension), renal issues (proteinuria, recurrent infections), neuropathy (tip-toe gait)
abnormal movements (restlessness, tip-toe gait), cognitive decline, psychiatric symptoms (vertigo, dyspnea can be psychological manifestations)
weight loss, joint pain (recurrent fractures), liver damage (hepatic steatosis, viral hepatitis), heart problems (cardiomyopathy, sick sinus syndrome)
muscle weakness and wasting, respiratory difficulties (dyspnea, respiratory insufficiency), enlarged heart (left ventricular hypertrophy)

connective tissue disorders causing joint instability leading to frequent injuries, vascular complications resulting in hemorrhage (hematochezia), possible gastrointestinal symptoms (abdominal pain, bloating)
lung function impairment (respiratory insufficiency, dyspnea), liver injury (steatohepatitis, cirrhosis)
easy bruising or bleeding tendency (hemorrhagic episodes including hematochezia), normal platelet count but prolonged clotting time
cafe au lait spots, freckling in axillary and inguinal regions, scoliosis, optic glioma, learning disabilities
